title: Flaky Canadian Pastry Pie
description: A comforting, easy-to-make pastry pie, perfect for a lazy Sunday morning. Filled with a delicious, tangy filling, this pie is sure to become a family favorite.

ingredients.name: Finely Chopped Onion
ingredients.notes: Soften the onion by saut5ing it and allowing it to cool before using.

instructions: Roll out the thawed shortcrust pastry to a thickness of 1/8 inch (3 mm).
Transfer the pastry to a 9-inch (23 cm) pie dish, pressing it into the corners and trimming the edges to fit.
Prick the bottom of the pastry with a fork and brush with melted butter.
In a large bowl, combine the cottage cheese, chopped onion, chopped fresh dill, grated black pepper, and salt. Mix well until combined.
Pour the cottage cheese mixture into the pastry-lined pie dish.
If using, sprinkle shredded cheddar cheese over the cottage cheese mixture.
In a small bowl, mix together the beaten egg, maple syrup, and a pinch of salt. Pour the egg mixture over the cottage cheese.
Roll out the remaining pastry to a thickness of 1/8 inch (3 mm). Use this pastry to cover the pie and crimp the edges to seal.
Cut a few slits in the top of the pastry to allow steam to escape during baking.
Brush the top of the pastry with melted butter and sprinkle with a pinch of salt.
Bake the pie for 40 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and the filling is set.
Serve warm, garnished with additional chopped fresh dill if desired.
